flashcards.definition: J-curve; population grows without limit: dN/dt = rN; occurs with unlimited resources.
S-curve; growth slows as population approaches carrying capacity: dN/dt = rN(1 − N/K).
Maximum population size an environment can sustain indefinitely given available resources.
Species producing many offspring with little parental care; rapid growth; short lifespan (insects, bacteria).
Species producing few offspring with extensive parental care; slow growth; long lifespan (elephants, whales).
